question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a sequenceNumber that duplicates an existing sequenceNumber for the same topicId, causing the CrudRepository save() to persist a duplicate sequence number, breaking the unique sequence invariant and causing downstream consumers to process the same logical message position twice?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: save() (CrudRepository)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage transaction with a message field containing a crafted byte sequence that triggers a heap buffer overflow in the PostgreSQL bytea type handler when the CrudRepository save() inserts the row, causing the database process to crash and denying service to all users?
